steps.title: Recognize the 3-4-5 Pythagorean triple
steps.content: Since $\cos A = \frac{3}{5}$ and $\sin^2 A + \cos^2 A = 1$, we have $\sin A = \frac{4}{5}$. In right triangle ABC with ∠B = 90°, the trigonometric ratios $\cos A = \frac{AB}{AC} = \frac{3}{5}$ and $\sin A = \frac{BC}{AC} = \frac{4}{5}$ match the 3-4-5 Pythagorean triple. Therefore, AB : BC : AC = 3 : 4 : 5.

steps.title: Find the scale factor
steps.content: BC = 136 corresponds to 4 in the 3-4-5 ratio. Setting $4k = 136$: $$k = 34$$

steps.title: Calculate AB and match the choice
steps.content: AB corresponds to 3 in the ratio: $$AB = 3 \cdot 34 = 102 \text{ mm}$$That matches choice B — B is correct.
